AAPL.rationale: P/E derating from 31x to 26.5x on improving margins (45% gross, 30.8% operating) creates a better entry despite China and DOJ headwinds that are largely priced in at 52-week lows.

Q1 FY2024 gross margin expanded to 45.0% (vs 43.1% FY2023) and operating margin to 30.8% (vs 29.4%) — fundamental trajectory improving

Stock at ~$171 near 52-week low of $169, analyst consensus target $211 — sentiment washed out for a high-quality franchise

trace.final_explanation: openclaw_per_ticker: 30/30 tickers produced a view via openclaw.

AMZN.final_explanation: Amazon's operating margin inflection from 2.4% to 6.4% (Q4 at 7.8%) with further runway justifies a mild long despite 62x trailing P/E, as earnings power is still expanding faster than the market has priced.

BA.final_explanation: Boeing remains deeply troubled with insolvent balance sheet and production crisis; prior bearish call was correct but stock has de-rated enough to reduce conviction on further downside.

CRM.final_explanation: CRM's profitability inflection is real but after a 123% run over 15 months and with P/E still at 72x, most upside is priced in — mild positive lean on continued margin expansion and AI monetization.

CVX.final_explanation: CVX's easy value recovery has played out — stock up 6% while P/E expanded from 11x to 14x on worse-than-expected earnings, leaving a fairly-priced oil bet with compressed margins and no clear edge.

GS.final_explanation: GS offers mild upside on capital markets recovery and strategy refocus, but the easy re-rating from deeply cheap valuations has largely played out — adjusted P/E has expanded from ~9x to ~13.5x, leaving less margin of safety.

HON.final_explanation: HON is a quality diversified industrial with improving fundamentals, but fair valuation, weak near-term guidance, and elevated acquisition integration risk offer no compelling edge over a one-year horizon.

INTC.final_explanation: INTC remains a compelling short: the market is paying 110x earnings for a company whose earnings power has collapsed 80% and whose turnaround (18A, foundry) is years from proving out, while China adds a new headwind.

JNJ.final_explanation: JNJ trades at a historical valuation discount (~16x vs 17-20x range) on a cleaner post-Kenvue business, with potential SWAV acquisition and favorable talc litigation developments as upside catalysts, though talc overhang persists.

KO.final_explanation: KO offers modestly attractive risk-adjusted reward: P/E has compressed from ~28x to ~24.8x despite 12.8% EPS growth, the business quality is intact (59.5% gross margin, 38.9% ROE), and the 3.2% dividend yield positions it well for eventual rate cuts — but it's still not cheap for 6% revenue growth.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD's excellent FY2023 fundamentals are not translating into stock gains — the persistent P/E de-rating from 34x to 24x despite 39% EPS growth signals the market is looking through to a slower growth future, making risk/reward slightly unfavorable.

NKE.final_explanation: NKE faces a genuine revenue trough with H1 FY25 guided to decline, but valuation has de-rated meaningfully (P/E 27x vs 33x prior), and the Olympic-driven innovation cycle creates asymmetric upside if new products land. The balance of risk vs reward remains slightly negative given the unproven turnaround.

V.final_explanation: Visa remains a world-class toll-booth with improving margins, but near all-time highs leave limited upside — mild positive lean maintained.

VZ.final_explanation: VZ offers income support via 6.4% yield and normalized PE ~9.2x, but revenue decline, Q4 earnings hit, and 37% rally from lows temper upside — mild long lean with moderate conviction.

WMT.final_explanation: WMT offers improving fundamentals (6% revenue growth, 32% operating income growth, expanding margins) at a still-reasonable ~10.5x pre-split PE, with a 3-for-1 stock split as a near-term catalyst — but the 14.5% rally since the prior decision three months ago has eroded much of the value cushion, warranting a modestly positive rather than strongly positive score.
